item.title: Keir Starmer hangs on to a disunited kingdom
item.description: The Labour leader is paying a price for not getting Britain’s fiscal house in order.

item.title: The latest trendy health food? The wellness industry has chosen.
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on wellness industry, food health trends.

item.title: Donald Trump’s incredibly reckless second term
item.description: A presidency stripped of restraints — and turned toward profit.

item.title: Is life better in Europe? It's not so simple.
item.description: Europe promises a life many Americans envy: longer vacations, universal health care, beautiful cities and a slower pace. But those benefits come with trade-offs. Host Megan McArdle looks past the usual America versus Europe arguments to explore what economic indicators reveal about quality of life.

item.title: We’re all famous and talented. Now what?
item.description: The AI-induced democratization of “talent” will hurt human imagination.

item.title: Never forget
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Memorial Day.

item.title: The bond markets are punishing America
item.description: Spiking Treasury yields are highlighting the unsustainability of federal spending.

item.title: This Ebola outbreak is a test the world doesn’t have to fail
item.description: The U.S. and international partners can act decisively to end this outbreak.

item.title: To save the midterms, Trump needs victory in Iran
item.description: Americans will endure high gas prices for a good military outcome — but not for a bad deal.

item.title: The Trump-Xi narrative isn’t true. America keeps falling uphill.
item.description: Hold off on those announcements of the dawn of the Chinese century.

item.title: At last, civics is making a college comeback. More, please.
item.description: Most colleges don't require a civics class to graduate, but students sorely need it.

item.title: What Thomas Massie's loss means for MAGA
item.description: After Thomas Massie's primary defeat, what does the future hold for the GOP and the MAGA movement? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Kate Andrews, James Hohmann and Robert Gebelhoff discuss the political cost of breaking from President Donald Trump and whether Republicans are speeding toward a midterms disaster.

item.title: The unintended consequences of mass deportations
item.description: The administration say immigration crackdowns are good for native-born workers. A new study shows otherwise.
